column5: @player1 let's build the core response team here. I've already added myself. Who else should we pull in to keep it tight and efficient?
I'd say one senior person each from security platform and engineering. Also add Tanya tasks could be platform intensive
Add Daniel Tanya
I'll get them here
Yes i'm in
Also Tinus is suggesting Cyvantics Security on retainer
Hello
I reckon we should loop them in sooner rather than later
Yes we've got an IR(incident response) retainer with Cyvantics Security
I've called in Hamed as well
Ok
Looped in Cyvantics Security - they're on their way now to take snapshots of the infected server and storage followed by forensics on it
Perfect
Meanwhile I've got this from Cyvantics. @player1 can you help track the steps and coordinate?
<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MThjMTk4ZDU5ODU0NGZmMWE1NzhmNGY0MDQwNWI1NTA>
We've got isolation steps in the playbook but before we dive in can you paste that list here? I'll help team run through it
Daniel What's our backups like?
Daniel has joined the channel
Daniel What's the backups looking like?
Haven't checked the backup in a while
Can you check now in case we need to roll back

Maya Steps for Isolation: Steps to be done mentioned along with the *owning* team 1. Disconnect the infected server -> Platforms2. Cut off infrastructure traffic -> Platforms 3. Disconnect DB & freeze backup -> Application 4. Detach from Shared Resource -> Platforms 5. Take snapshots (Disk & Memory) -> Ask team 6. Kill suspicious process or activity -> Platforms
I've given write access to Tanya Hamed Daniel and myself
Disconnecting the checkout servers from the infra now
I'm on it. Disconnecting DB access for checkout and checking if scheduled backups exist
I'll verify if any shared storage is still attached to the checkout DB
Sure
Perfect thanks all
Step 3 done DB connection to checkout is now cut
Step 1 done Checkout servers are now fully disconnected. Moving to isolate the infra from all external traffic
Step 4 confirmed No shared storage attached to checkout which makes sense since checkout has its standalone storage
Step 2 completed Infra is now fully cut off from the outside world. That means the website will remain completely down until we recover
Step 5 done Cyvantics has completed taking snapshots of the checkout servers and database. They've now moved on to forensics. @Hamed we're good to kill the script now
Step 6 done The Script has been killed. @player1 let us know and double-check if anything else is pending on our side
@player1 just double-checking with you are we good with restoration?
Sorry Isolation
How are we checking? Just that the site is down?
Yeah site would be down because I cut off the complete infrastructure from the network
Okay yes I'm getting 502s
Tanya Is there anything else to check is it all isolated now?
Tanya has joined the channel
I think we are good for isolation
Yes we must have a backup. Platform team can confirm us
Okay then lets move onto restoration
Correct we do have backup which only runs at 2AM. Latest is yesterday's. We'll lose today's orders
Isolation is complete. We're good to move toward restoration using the backup storage now
@player1 could you please confirm with business before moving forward on this
I've called in @Bob to the channel
@player1 I hear you need my help what exactly should I look into?
With the sale starting today the potential loss is significant - around $180k
Maya confirming now
What's the alternative though we keep losing more sales?
Cyvantics has the infected data. But it's gonna take a long time to recover it
And it's not easy to sanitize it
Okay so we can potentially restore that after right Maya
How many systems ended up getting infected Maya
We do have the potential but its very very risky
Checking...
From what I'm seeing the impact seems limited to the checkout servers. We haven't spotted similar symptoms on the others so far
Also I checked. All other servers have security agents running and reporting normally. The only ones missing agents were the checkout servers - which feels a bit off
Okay
If that's the case can we restore just the checkout service?
It would mean not losing those lost sales right?
Yes I'll coordinate. Just have a track of it
Or am I misunderstanding the architecture Tanya
I'd say we forget about getting the checkout data right now. Getting data out of infected storage isn't easy and reliable
Okay
Agreed and business is onboard with that we'll go through insurance
Let's start the restoration
Ok then shall we move to restoration?
Yes
Thanks for the go-ahead. @player1 please track all restoration steps
Daniel Can you validate the backups for us
I'm now validating the backup storage to ensure it's safe
I'm provisioning new checkout servers will update once ready
Backup is clean and safe. @Hamed you can start rebuilding checkout from backup storage
Thanks Maya. New servers are ready. I'll start rebuilding now using backup storage
Rebuild completed
I've enabled the security agents (EDR) on checkout servers
I'll monitor application health once services are restored
Maya has done it
@player1  can you confirm all tasks are wrapped up and we are good to bring the traffic back? Just want to be sure as this is the final restoration step before we move
Okay make sure that EDR is running stable
Yes 100%
Okay then1 is done 2 is done 3 is done 4 is done
Go ahead and reconnect Hamed S
Tanya  please move ahead
Thanks for the go ahead. Bringing up network traffic in progress...
Restoration completed
Wait!! getting a bunch of new complaints now. Users are neither able to see their return requests nor able to place new returns on the site
Yeah that's probably the missing today's checkout data. Anyone who ordered or returned something during that time won't see it - makes sense since we restored from the yesterday's backup
Tough but expected :)
What should I tell them? They are asking for refunds
Let me ask business
Bez is checking with the board Bob
Bob has joined the channel
@player1 I've received the forensics report from Cyvantics Security
forensics
Okay thanks for that
Also I just finished combing through Shay's mailbox. There's something important here I found two emails from what looks like our monitoring vendor - ClearTech
phished_email
Okay that looks like the cause then
We will take this forward in PIR  for now website is restored
Alright since everything's back up and stable we can now move back to the main #online-boutique channel. No further actions left here
What should I tell the customers?
Bob From Bez Keeping it high-level just say website restored but there's an issue with few orders. We'll refund them and customers will get a $10 gift voucher as an apology
Thanks @player1
